title: AI-Driven Software Development Maturity Model
summary_markdown: This model describes how software development evolves as agency transfers from humans to AI-driven systems. The primary axis of progress is **agency in the development loop**, supported by secondary improvement dimensions and constrained by fate-determining system choices.

primary_axis.name: Agency in the Development Loop
primary_axis.definition_markdown: Agency describes **who decides what happens next** in the software development loop (observe → decide → act → evaluate).
primary_axis.why_primary_markdown:
Agency is the most important axis because it defines the **binding constraint** of the system.

- Every development system has a constraint: either human cognition or system evaluation.
- Improvements in parallelism or self-improvement increase capacity **within** the current constraint but do not move it.
- Changing agency changes **what kinds of failures are possible**, and therefore **what kinds of learning can occur**.

> Parallelism changes *how much* work happens.
> Self-improvement changes *how fast* behavior evolves.
> Agency changes *who is allowed to be wrong next*.

Because of this, agency determines where discomfort lives, which identities dissolve, and what must be learned to progress.
primary_axis.emotional_frame_markdown:
People do not resist AI because they fear job loss. They resist it because **identities dissolve before roles disappear**.

This axis makes visible:
- which responsibilities vanish,
- when they vanish,
- and what remains essentially human.

Progress requires consciously trading **situational awareness** for **leverage**.

secondary_axes.name: System Self-Improvement
secondary_axes.definition_markdown: Whether and how the system modifies its own behavior over time based on observation and evaluation.

secondary_axes.name: Parallelism
secondary_axes.definition_markdown: How many alternative futures or work streams the system can explore simultaneously.

fate_determining_choices.name: System of Record / Data Gravity
fate_determining_choices.definition_markdown: Where authoritative state lives. Systems that keep all durable state in source control enable branching, inspection, replay, and self-improvement. Tool-centric systems cap out early.

fate_determining_choices.name: Determinism Boundary
fate_determining_choices.definition_markdown: Which operations must be correct and repeatable (deterministic) versus exploratory and probabilistic.

fate_determining_choices.name: Evaluation Explicitness
fate_determining_choices.definition_markdown: How correctness and quality are represented, ranging from implicit human judgment to executable tests and metrics.

fate_determining_choices.name: Memory Strategy
fate_determining_choices.definition_markdown: What the system remembers, for how long, and why. Excess memory without pruning causes incoherence; too little prevents learning.
